metadata.website_type: b2b saas website for ai customer communications

tasks.name: Configure an AI website chatbot to greet US visitors and capture 3 lead fields
tasks.description: Set up a new AI website chatbot that only appears to visitors from the United States, greets them with a custom message, and collects full name, company, and work email before routing high-budget leads to the Sales inbox. Success criteria: a bot named 'Website Lead Assistant' is activated on the 'Production Website' channel, targets US visitors only, asks 3 required questions, and routes leads with budgets over $5,000 to Sales.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage and click on the main navigation item labeled 'Bots' or 'Chatbots'.
Click the 'New Bot' or 'Create Bot' button and select the 'Website chatbot' type with the 'Lead Generation' template if available.
In the bot name field, type 'Website Lead Assistant' and select 'Production Website' from the 'Channel' dropdown.
Open the 'Audience' or 'Targeting' section, set the base audience to 'All visitors', then add a condition where 'Country' equals 'United States'.
In the greeting message editor, replace any default text with: 'Hi! I’m your virtual assistant. I can help with pricing and demos. First, what’s your full name, company, and work email?'.

In the conversation flow builder, add three sequential question steps labeled 'Full name', 'Company name', and 'Work email', and mark each of these three questions as 'Required' using the required/mandatory toggle.

Add a numeric question titled 'Estimated monthly budget (USD)' and configure an action that, when the answer is 'More than $5,000', assigns the conversation to the 'Sales' inbox; for any other value, keep the default 'General' inbox assignment.

Open the 'Availability' or 'Business Hours' settings, set hours to Monday through Friday, 9:00 to 17:00, choose the 'America/New_York' timezone, and select a predefined offline message such as 'We’re offline right now, but leave your details and we’ll get back to you.' from the message dropdown.
In the 'Behavior' or 'Targeting rules' section, enable the toggle 'Show bot to new visitors only' and set the 'Show again after' or 'Repeat after' value to '30 days'.
Click the 'Save & Activate' or 'Publish' button to activate the 'Website Lead Assistant' bot on the 'Production Website' channel.

tasks.name: Create an AI email auto-reply for new urgent support tickets within 10 minutes
tasks.description: Configure an automation that sends an AI-generated email reply to any new email conversation tagged 'urgent' within 10 minutes, using a standardized subject line and dynamic placeholders. Success criteria: an active automation exists that triggers on new email conversations with the 'urgent' tag, waits up to 10 minutes, then sends an AI-drafted email from 'support@demo-company.test' with '[URGENT]' in the subject.
steps: From the main navigation, click on 'Automations' or 'Workflows' and then click the 'New Automation' or 'Create Rule' button.
Select the trigger type 'New conversation created' and set the channel condition so that 'Channel' equals 'Email'.
Add a condition that the conversation 'Tags' field includes the tag 'urgent' by selecting 'Tags contains' and typing 'urgent'.

In the actions section, add an action of type 'Generate reply with AI' or 'Ask AI to draft email', and choose the template named 'Urgent support acknowledgement' from the template dropdown if available.
Edit the subject line field of the email action to: '[URGENT] We’ve received your request (Ticket {{ticket_id}})' and keep the dynamic placeholder for ticket ID if present.

In the email body editor, type a short message that includes placeholders for the customer’s first name and ticket ID, for example: 'Hi {{customer_first_name}}, we’ve logged your urgent request (Ticket {{ticket_id}}). Our team will respond shortly.'
Set the sender address by choosing or typing 'support@demo-company.test' in the 'From' address field or dropdown.
Add a 'Wait' or 'Delay' step before the send action and set the delay duration to '10 minutes' so that the reply is scheduled within that timeframe.
Enable the option 'Send automatically without human review' or equivalent, so the AI-generated email is sent automatically when conditions match.
Click 'Save' and toggle the automation status to 'Active' so the urgent email auto-reply rule is turned on.

tasks.name: Route pricing and technical issue messages to the correct inboxes with priorities
tasks.description: Create a routing rule that assigns any conversation mentioning 'price', 'pricing', or 'quote' to the Sales inbox with normal priority, and any conversation mentioning 'bug', 'error', or 'not working' to the Support inbox with high priority. Success criteria: one active rule named 'Pricing vs Technical Routing' that evaluates on the first customer message, routes pricing-related messages to Sales with normal priority, and technical issue messages to Support with high priority.
steps: Open the main navigation, click on 'Settings', and then select the 'Routing' or 'Inbox Rules' section.
Click the 'New rule' or 'Add routing rule' button and enter 'Pricing vs Technical Routing' in the rule name field.
Create the first condition group by selecting 'Message text' as the field, 'contains any of' as the operator, and entering three separate keywords: 'price', 'pricing', and 'quote'.
For this first condition group, add an action that sets 'Assigned inbox' to 'Sales' using the inbox dropdown and sets 'Priority' to 'Normal' using the priority dropdown.

Add a second condition group by clicking 'Add condition group', set the logic connector between groups to 'OR', and configure the new group so 'Message text' 'contains any of' the keywords 'bug', 'error', and 'not working'.
For the second condition group, add an action that sets 'Assigned inbox' to 'Support' and sets 'Priority' to 'High' using the appropriate dropdowns.
Locate the 'When to apply' or 'Evaluation' settings for the rule and select 'On first customer message only' from the options.
In the rule schedule or availability section, choose 'Always on' and set the region or site scope to 'Global' or 'All workspaces' if a scope selector is available.
Drag the 'Pricing vs Technical Routing' rule to the top of the routing rules list so it is evaluated before other rules, then click the 'Save' button.
Toggle the rule status to 'Active' if needed so that the 'Pricing vs Technical Routing' rule is turned on.

tasks.name: Build an analytics report for 30-day AI resolution rate by channel
tasks.description: Create a saved analytics report that shows AI resolution rate for each channel over the last 30 days, filtered to conversations where AI handled at least 80% of the messages, sorted by the number of AI-resolved conversations from highest to lowest. Success criteria: a saved report named 'AI Resolution - 30 days' exists with a 30-day date range, AI coverage filter of 80% or more, grouped by channel, and sorted by AI-resolved conversation count descending.
steps: Navigate to the 'Analytics' or 'Reports' section from the main navigation and click on 'New report' or 'Create report'.
Choose a report type such as 'Conversations' or 'AI performance' and set the primary metric to 'AI resolution rate' using the metric dropdown.
Open the date range selector and select 'Last 30 days' as the reporting period.
In the 'Filters' panel, add a filter for 'AI messages percentage' or 'AI coverage', set the operator to 'Greater than or equal to', and enter the value '80'.
In the 'Group by' dropdown, select 'Channel' so that data is broken down by channels like Chat, Email, and WhatsApp.
Add a secondary metric to the report for 'AI-resolved conversations' by selecting it from the metrics list or columns settings.
Open the 'Sort' options and set the primary sort to 'AI-resolved conversations' in 'Descending' order so that the channel with the most AI-resolved conversations appears first.
Click into the 'Report name' field, type 'AI Resolution - 30 days', and optionally add 'AI coverage ≥ 80%' in the description or notes field.
Click the 'Save report' button to store the configuration, and then click 'Pin to dashboard' or 'Add to dashboard' so that the report appears on the main analytics dashboard.
Return to the dashboard view and drag the 'AI Resolution - 30 days' widget to the top row of the layout so it is prominently displayed.

tasks.name: Connect a mock CRM integration and map key contact fields
tasks.description: Set up the built-in 'Demo CRM Sandbox' integration and map email, full name, and company fields between the AI platform and the CRM. Success criteria: the 'Demo CRM Sandbox' integration is connected using API key 'TEST-1234', and field mappings are configured so 'Customer email' maps to 'Contact Email', 'Customer full name' maps to 'Contact Name', and 'Company name' maps to 'Account Name'.
steps: From the main navigation, click on 'Settings' and then select the 'Integrations' or 'Apps' section.
Locate the integration labeled 'Demo CRM Sandbox' in the list of available integrations and click the 'Connect' or 'Configure' button next to it.
In the connection form, find the 'API key' or 'Access token' field and type 'TEST-1234' into that field.
If there is an environment selector, choose 'Sandbox' or 'Test' from the 'Environment' dropdown.
Click the 'Next' or 'Continue' button to move to the field mapping step for the CRM integration.
In the mapping table, set the platform field 'Customer email' to map to the CRM field 'Contact Email' by selecting it from the corresponding dropdown.
Set the platform field 'Customer full name' to map to the CRM field 'Contact Name' using the mapping dropdown in the same table.
Set the platform field 'Company name' to map to the CRM field 'Account Name', again using the field selector or dropdown.
Enable the toggle or checkbox for 'Sync new conversations to CRM' or 'Auto-create contacts' so that new conversations create or update CRM records.
Click the 'Save' or 'Enable integration' button to finish connecting 'Demo CRM Sandbox' with the configured mappings.

tasks.name: Create a knowledge base with 3 AI-usable FAQ articles and enable it for AI answers
tasks.description: Add three public FAQ articles to the knowledge base with the keywords 'pricing', 'trial', and 'support hours' in their titles and enable the knowledge base as a source for AI-generated answers in chat. Success criteria: three articles exist titled with those keywords, all marked as public, and the 'Use knowledge base for AI answers' toggle is turned on for chatbots.
steps: Click on 'Knowledge Base' or 'Help Center' in the main navigation and then click the 'New article' or 'Create article' button.
For the first article, type the title 'Pricing and plans overview' and select 'Public' as the visibility option using the visibility dropdown or toggle.
In the content editor for the first article, add at least one paragraph describing the pricing tiers and include the word 'pricing' at least once in the body text, then click 'Save' or 'Publish'.

Create a second article by clicking 'New article' again, set the title to 'How to start a free trial', mark the article as 'Public', and write a short paragraph that includes the word 'trial' before saving it.

Create a third article with the title 'Our support hours and response times', select 'Public' visibility, and add content that explicitly mentions 'support hours' before saving or publishing the article.
Return to the main Knowledge Base settings by clicking 'Settings' or 'Knowledge Base settings' within the knowledge base section.
Locate the option labeled 'Use knowledge base for AI answers' or similar under the AI or Chatbot subsection and enable the toggle so it is turned on.
In the same settings panel, set the 'Preferred source for AI answers' option to 'Knowledge base first, then conversation history' or the closest available option from the dropdown.
Click the 'Save settings' or 'Apply changes' button to confirm the AI knowledge base usage configuration.

Optionally, navigate back to the 'Bots' section, open the configuration of an existing bot, and ensure the 'Knowledge base' is selected as a content source in its AI settings by choosing the main knowledge base from the data source dropdown.

tasks.name: Add a new support agent user and restrict access to the Support inbox
tasks.description: Create a new user with the Agent role who can only access the Support inbox and has working hours set to weekdays 8:00–16:00. Success criteria: a user with email 'alex.support@example.test' exists, has the role 'Agent', is assigned only to the 'Support' inbox, and has working hours configured as Monday–Friday, 08:00–16:00.
steps: Open the 'Settings' menu from the main navigation and click on 'Users' or 'Team'.
Click the 'Invite user' or 'Add user' button and enter 'Alex Support' in the 'Full name' field.
In the email field, type 'alex.support@example.test' and select 'Agent' from the 'Role' dropdown.
In the 'Inboxes' or 'Queues' assignment section, deselect all inboxes except 'Support' so that only 'Support' remains checked or selected.
If there is a 'Permissions' or 'Access level' panel, ensure options for 'View all conversations' are disabled and that only 'Assigned inboxes' is selected.
Locate the 'Working hours' or 'Schedule' section for the user and enable custom working hours for this user.
Set the working days to Monday through Friday by checking or selecting these five days and leave Saturday and Sunday unselected.
For each selected weekday, set the start time to '08:00' and the end time to '16:00' using the time selectors or input fields.
Click 'Send invite', 'Create user', or 'Save' to finalize the creation of the user 'Alex Support' with the specified inbox and schedule.
Optionally, navigate to the 'Support' inbox settings, open the 'Team members' tab, and confirm that 'Alex Support' is listed there by ensuring the checkbox next to their name is checked.

tasks.name: Choose the cheapest annual plan under $400/month that includes AI and at least 5 seats
tasks.description: From the pricing page, select the annual subscription plan that costs less than $400 per month, includes AI assistant features, and supports at least 5 teammate seats, then start the trial flow for that plan. Success criteria: the chosen plan is the cheapest option meeting all three conditions (annual billing, price per month under $400, AI features included, and at least 5 seats) and the trial setup form is started with company size set to '51–100 employees'.
steps: Navigate to the 'Pricing' page using the main navigation menu and ensure the billing toggle is set to 'Annual' or 'Yearly' instead of 'Monthly'.
Review the listed plans and identify all plans that show a monthly equivalent price under '$400' when billed annually by looking at each plan's price text.
From those under-$400 annual plans, further narrow down to plans that explicitly list 'AI assistant' or 'AI customer communication' in their feature list.
From this narrowed list, select the plan that includes at least '5 seats' or '5 teammates' by checking the seats or user count indicated in each plan’s details.

If more than one plan meets all three conditions (annual, under $400/month, AI features included, at least 5 seats), choose the one with the lowest monthly equivalent price by comparing the displayed amounts and click the 'Start trial' or 'Get started' button on that plan.
On the trial signup form that opens, fill in the 'Company name' field with 'Example AI Customer Co'.
Locate the 'Company size' or 'Number of employees' dropdown and choose the range '51–100 employees' from the available options.
Enter a work email in the email field, using 'owner@example.test' as the value, and type 'Owner' in any 'Job title' or 'Role' field present.
If there is a 'Use AI features' or 'Enable AI assistant' checkbox in the form, ensure it is checked or turned on.
Click the 'Continue', 'Create account', or 'Start trial' button at the bottom of the form to begin the trial process for the selected annual plan.

entities.name: Channel
entities.description: Customer-facing communication channels such as Production Website, Email, WhatsApp.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name, e.g. 'Production Website'

entities.name: Inbox
entities.description: Work queues like Sales, Support, General that conversations can be routed to.

fields.name: name
fields.description: e.g. 'Sales', 'Support', 'General'

fields.name: channel_ids
fields.description: IDs of channels whose conversations typically arrive in this inbox

entities.name: Bot
entities.description: AI bot configuration for a single channel (e.g., Website Lead Assistant).

fields.name: channel_id
fields.description: ID of Channel such as 'Production Website'

fields.name: template_name
fields.description: e.g. 'Lead Generation'

fields.name: audience_rule_ids
fields.description: IDs of TargetingRule objects controlling visibility

fields.name: flow_step_ids
fields.description: Ordered list of BotFlowStep IDs in the conversation flow

fields.name: business_hours
fields.description: Array of objects like {day_of_week, start_time, end_time}

fields.name: timezone
fields.description: e.g. 'America/New_York'

fields.name: offline_message_template_id
fields.description: ID of ChatMessageTemplate for offline message

fields.name: show_to_new_visitors_only
fields.description: Toggle 'Show bot to new visitors only'

fields.name: repeat_after_days
fields.description: How many days before showing bot again to same visitor

fields.name: use_knowledge_base
fields.description: Whether the bot can use KB for AI answers

entities.name: BotFlowStep
entities.description: Individual steps in a bot conversation flow such as questions and actions.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Display label e.g. 'Full name'

fields.name: field_key
fields.description: Internal capture key e.g. 'full_name'

fields.name: order
fields.description: Position in flow

fields.name: condition_actions
fields.description: Array of objects defining conditional actions based on answer, e.g. [{comparison_operator: 'greater_than', comparison_value: 5000, action_type: 'assign_inbox', target_inbox_id, target_priority}]

entities.name: TargetingRule
entities.description: Audience/targeting configuration for a bot.

fields.name: condition_group_id
fields.description: Primary ConditionGroup defining filters like country = United States

entities.name: ConditionGroup
entities.description: A group of conditions combined with AND/OR, used by targeting, automations, routing, and reports.

fields.name: condition_ids
fields.description: IDs of Condition objects

entities.name: Condition
entities.description: Single conditional expression used in rules and filters.

fields.name: field
fields.description: e.g. 'country', 'channel', 'tags', 'message_text', 'ai_coverage'

fields.name: value
fields.description: Single comparison value

fields.name: values
fields.description: Multiple comparison values, e.g. ['price','pricing','quote']

entities.name: ChatMessageTemplate
entities.description: Predefined chat messages such as offline messages or greetings.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Internal label

entities.name: AutomationRule
entities.description: Automation/workflow rule such as urgent email auto-reply.

fields.name: condition_group_ids
fields.description: Top-level condition groups controlling when automation runs

fields.name: action_ids
fields.description: IDs of AutomationAction steps in execution order

entities.name: AutomationAction
entities.description: Individual step in an automation such as wait, generate AI reply, or send email.

fields.name: email_template_id
fields.description: EmailTemplate used for AI-drafted replies

fields.name: send_without_review
fields.description: Auto-send AI reply without human approval

fields.name: delay_minutes
fields.description: Used when action_type is 'wait'

fields.name: position
fields.description: Execution order within rule

entities.name: EmailTemplate
entities.description: AI-usable email templates, e.g., 'Urgent support acknowledgement'.

fields.name: use_ai_generation
fields.description: Whether AI drafts from this template

entities.name: Conversation
entities.description: Customer conversation or ticket across channels.

fields.name: ticket_id
fields.description: Human-facing ticket identifier used in email templates

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Free-form tags such as 'urgent'

fields.name: first_customer_message_text
fields.description: Used by routing rules on first message

fields.name: messages
fields.description: Array of message objects with sender, text, timestamps

fields.name: ai_messages_percentage
fields.description: Percentage of messages authored by AI

fields.name: budget_estimate
fields.description: Estimated monthly budget captured by bot

fields.name: source_bot_id
fields.description: Bot that created the lead, if any

entities.name: RoutingRule
entities.description: Rules that route conversations to inboxes with priorities based on message content.

fields.name: condition_group_ids
fields.description: Condition groups representing branches like pricing vs technical

fields.name: branch_actions
fields.description: Array of objects mapping condition_group_id to actions, e.g. [{condition_group_id, assign_inbox_id, priority}]

fields.name: order
fields.description: Rule position in evaluation list

entities.name: AnalyticsReport
entities.description: Saved analytics report definitions such as 'AI Resolution - 30 days'.

fields.name: secondary_metrics
fields.description: Array of metric identifiers, e.g. ['ai_resolved_conversations']

fields.name: filter_condition_group_id
fields.description: ConditionGroup referencing filters such as AI coverage >= 80

fields.name: group_by
fields.description: Array of dimensions such as ['channel','inbox','agent','bot']

fields.name: sort_by_field
fields.description: e.g. 'ai_resolved_conversations'

entities.name: DashboardWidget
entities.description: Pinned analytics widgets and their layout positions on the analytics dashboard.

fields.name: row
fields.description: Row position on dashboard grid (0-based or 1-based)

fields.name: column
fields.description: Column position on dashboard grid

fields.name: width
fields.description: Width in grid columns

entities.name: Integration
entities.description: External integration configuration, e.g., Demo CRM Sandbox.

fields.name: sync_new_conversations
fields.description: Toggle 'Sync new conversations to CRM'

fields.name: auto_create_contacts
fields.description: Toggle 'Auto-create contacts'

entities.name: IntegrationFieldMapping
entities.description: Field mappings between platform data and external CRM fields.

fields.name: platform_field
fields.description: e.g. 'Customer email', 'Customer full name', 'Company name'

fields.name: crm_field
fields.description: e.g. 'Contact Email', 'Contact Name', 'Account Name'

entities.name: KnowledgeBase
entities.description: Knowledge base container used for help center and AI answers.

entities.name: KnowledgeBaseArticle
entities.description: FAQ/help articles such as pricing, trial, and support hours.

fields.name: keywords
fields.description: Extracted keywords, e.g. ['pricing','trial','support hours']

entities.name: KnowledgeBaseSettings
entities.description: Settings controlling how AI uses the knowledge base for answers.

fields.name: use_for_ai_answers
fields.description: Toggle 'Use knowledge base for AI answers'

fields.name: apply_to_bots
fields.description: Whether bots should use these KB settings

entities.name: Agent
entities.description: Team member profiles (e.g., support agents) with roles, inbox access, and working hours.

fields.name: assigned_inbox_ids
fields.description: IDs of inboxes this agent can access (e.g. Support inbox only)

fields.name: permissions
fields.description: Array of permission enums such as 'assigned_inboxes_only', 'view_all_conversations', 'manage_routing', 'manage_bots'

fields.name: working_hours
fields.description: For custom hours, array of {day_of_week, start_time, end_time}; e.g. Monday–Friday 08:00–16:00

entities.name: PricingPlan
entities.description: Subscription plans shown on Pricing page.

fields.name: price_per_month
fields.description: Displayed equivalent monthly price under the billing mode

fields.name: original_annual_price
fields.description: Total annual price if billed annually

fields.name: includes_ai_assistant
fields.description: Whether AI assistant / AI customer communication features are included

fields.name: included_seats
fields.description: Number of teammates/seats included

entities.name: TrialSignup
entities.description: Trial signup records capturing selected plan and company info.

fields.name: use_ai_features
fields.description: Checkbox such as 'Use AI assistant'
